method: WebSearch + WebFetch only, NO contact, NO booking
honest_c3: All facility specifics labeled PUBLIC/INFERRED/UNKNOWN. No facility was contacted; lab-share availability and rates are estimated from analogous Korean university facilities or sister institutions in literature.

facilities.name: KAIST Brain Interfaces and Neural Prosthetics Lab (BINP)
facilities.department: Department of Brain and Cognitive Sciences (BCS)
facilities.tms_device_model: UNKNOWN — lab page mentions Deep Brain Stimulation + Electric/Magnetic Neural Stimulation; specific TMS vendor not disclosed publicly
facilities.scheduling_lead_time_weeks: ESTIMATE 8–12 weeks (typical KAIST external collab MoU)
facilities.irb_pathway: KAIST IRB (or user's home institution IRB with cross-recognition; users without university affiliation = blocker — KAIST IRB does not accept individual non-affiliated researchers)
facilities.korean_only_pi: INFERRED Y — KAIST labs typically require Korean-affiliated PI or formal MoU; foreign individual researcher access is unusual
facilities.notes: Lab focus = neural prosthetics + DBS + magnetic neural stim, not consciousness/PCI specifically. Could be willing collaborator if framed as joint method paper, but session-as-a-service model unlikely.

facilities.name: KAIST Brain x Machine Intelligence Lab
facilities.department: Department of Brain and Cognitive Sciences (BCS) / KI for Health Science and Technology
facilities.tms_device_model: UNKNOWN — research page confirms 'TMS and tDCS' methodology, vendor unspecified publicly. Likely MagVenture MagPro or Magstim Rapid² (most common in Korean uni research labs)
facilities.byoe_compatibility: UNKNOWN — but lab uses EEG (model-based fMRI+EEG combined work); custom EEG amp integration plausible at engineering level
facilities.irb_pathway: KAIST IRB; same individual-researcher blocker as F1
facilities.notes: Best methodological fit (TMS+EEG+computational neuro), but unclear PCI experience. Higher chance of co-author MoU than rental.

facilities.name: Yonsei University Medical School / Severance Hospital — multiple TMS-active depts
facilities.director: Multiple PIs across depts; no single TMS-EEG core PI publicly identified

contact.phone: Yonsei MED main 1599-1004; Severance dept switchboard

facilities.tms_device_model: PUBLIC — Yonsei brain research uses fMRI, PET/CT, MEG, EEG, TMS, tACS (lab page mention). Specific TMS vendor not disclosed; literature shows Magstim and MagVenture both used in Severance studies.
facilities.byoe_compatibility: INFERRED N — clinical-grade TMS suites typically require their own integrated EEG (e.g., NeurOne, BrainAmp DC) for safety and CE-mark compliance. Custom OpenBCI integration unlikely without long IRB amendment.
facilities.lab_share_rate_krw_per_session: UNKNOWN — clinical rTMS treatment ~150,000–250,000 KRW/session per public clinic data
facilities.lab_share_rate_usd_per_session: ESTIMATE $500–900/session for research-grade slot (higher than uni labs due to hospital overhead)
facilities.scheduling_lead_time_weeks: ESTIMATE 12–24 weeks (hospital IRB + dept review + slot allocation)
facilities.irb_pathway: Yonsei/Severance IRB (hospital-based IRB stricter than university IRB; foreign or non-affiliated researcher cross-IRB recognition path possible but slow)
facilities.korean_only_pi: INFERRED Y for clinical, possibly N for research-only (foreign collaborator under Korean co-PI is common)
facilities.individual_researcher_access: BLOCKED for solo non-affiliated user; possible via Korean co-PI MoU
facilities.notes: Clinical-grade environment = highest signal quality but highest barrier. PCI Massimini protocol likely already known to Severance neurology team. Best for high-N validation cohort, not solo n=1 capex-light pilot.

facilities.name: Korea University Affective Cognitive Neuroscience Lab
facilities.department: Department of Brain and Cognitive Engineering

contact.email: UNKNOWN per faculty profile (not on dept lab listing page)

facilities.tms_device_model: PUBLIC — lab page confirms 'rTMS/tCS' methodology. Vendor unspecified publicly. Published work (Kim et al., Stroop task, n=high-frequency rTMS) typically uses Magstim Rapid² or MagVenture MagPro in Korean affective neuro labs.
facilities.byoe_compatibility: INFERRED N for hard integration; INFERRED Y if user runs OpenBCI in parallel as separate stream (no TMS-amp synchronization, uses TTL trigger). Lab combines fMRI/rTMS/tCS/EEG/ERP — has EEG infrastructure they'd default to.
facilities.scheduling_lead_time_weeks: ESTIMATE 6–12 weeks (Korea U IRB faster than hospital IRB)
facilities.irb_pathway: Korea U IRB (faster than Severance); BUT external non-affiliated solo researcher = same blocker. Co-PI MoU required.
facilities.korean_only_pi: INFERRED Y — Korea U external researcher policy not publicly documented for paid lab-share; likely requires Korean co-PI
facilities.individual_researcher_access: BLOCKED solo; PARTIAL via Korean co-PI
facilities.notes: TOP CANDIDATE — most explicit public mention of rTMS+EEG combined, dept located in Seoul (transport easier than Daejeon for most users), Korea U IRB lead time shorter than hospital IRB. Lab focus = affective cognition (not consciousness/PCI), but methodologically very compatible.

facilities.name: Seoul National University Department of Psychiatry
facilities.department: Department of Psychiatry, SNU College of Medicine; SNUH Neuropsychiatry
facilities.director: Prof. Jun Soo Kwon (권준수) — schizophrenia + TMS PI

contact.source: PUBLIC — SNU College of Medicine personal page

facilities.tms_device_model: UNKNOWN publicly. SNU psychiatry literature on TMS in schizophrenia uses Magstim Rapid² in published methods (e.g., 2024 KJSR paper). MagVenture also present elsewhere in SNUH.
facilities.byoe_compatibility: INFERRED N — clinical psychiatry setting, TMS used as treatment + research. Custom 16ch BYOE integration would require dedicated IRB amendment.
facilities.lab_share_rate_krw_per_session: UNKNOWN — clinical TMS treatment in SNUH ~200,000 KRW/session; research slot likely similar or higher
facilities.scheduling_lead_time_weeks: ESTIMATE 16–28 weeks (SNUH IRB slowest among Big-5; SNUH IRB intake = e-IRB system, requires SNU/SNUH affiliation for primary applicant)
facilities.irb_pathway: SNUH IRB (e-IRB) — only Korean-affiliated PI. External individual cannot file directly; must go through SNUH co-PI delegation.
facilities.korean_only_pi: Y — confirmed SNUH IRB requires Korean-affiliated PI; foreign individual researchers accepted only via co-PI delegation
facilities.individual_researcher_access: BLOCKED solo; possible via SNUH co-PI MoU only
facilities.notes: Highest prestige + best-known TMS PI (Kwon), but slowest IRB and tightest gating. Suitable for high-N peer-reviewed publication track, not for $1.6–2.5K one-shot pilot.

facilities.name: Pusan National University — Brain Stimulation System Laboratory
facilities.city: Yangsan campus (PNU School of Korean Medicine)
facilities.director: Prof. Shin (신 교수, lab name shinlab)
facilities.tms_device_model: OUT-OF-SCOPE — lab focus = upconversion-nanoparticle near-infrared brain stimulation (optical, not TMS). NOT a TMS facility.

facilities.name: KIST Brain Science Institute (BSI) — Neurocomputing Lab / Bionics Research Center
facilities.tms_device_model: UNKNOWN — BSI focuses on BCI, brain disease, brain cognitive research. TMS not advertised as core capability. Joint research with Severance/Korea U/Samsung Medical Center confirmed = could broker access via partner hospital, but not direct provider.
facilities.byoe_compatibility: INFERRED Y for partner-brokered route — KIST is government research institute, more flexible than hospital IRB
facilities.scope_match: PARTIAL — broker route only, not direct

facilities.name: KRISS Korea Research Institute of Standards and Science — Biomagnetism / Quantum Magnetic Sensing
facilities.department: Quantum Magnetic Sensing Group, Quantum Technology Institute
facilities.director: Yong-Ho Lee (PI), Jeong Shim (Quantum Magnetic Imaging Team)
facilities.tms_device_model: NO TMS at KRISS — KRISS has 152-ch MEG (SQUID/DROS-based) for biomagnetic measurement only. No TMS facility advertised. (Per N-14 MEG fallback note: KRISS = MEG-only.)
facilities.scope_match: N for TMS; Y for separate MEG fallback (already documented in N-14)

facilities.name: Hanyang University ERICA — Cognitive Science Institute
facilities.tms_device_model: UNKNOWN — public web search did not surface ERICA cognitive science institute TMS facility. Mentioned in N-19 spec §5.4 but no public confirmation of active TMS lab.
facilities.scope_match: UNKNOWN — needs deeper local-language search or direct inquiry

byoe_global_finding: TMS-EEG combined research field has NO established standard for low-cost BYOE integration. Massimini-grade PCI requires TMS-compatible EEG amplifier (sample-and-hold or DC-coupled high-dynamic-range, e.g., NeurOne, BrainAmp DC, eXimia, Nexstim eXimia EEG). 16ch OpenBCI Cyton+Daisy lacks sample-and-hold = TMS pulse saturates amp for 5–50 ms post-pulse, destroying early TEP (0–30 ms) which is the most clinically diagnostic window. Mitigation = post-hoc ICA + extended baseline, but accepts lower SNR. NO Korean lab publicly advertises BYOE policy for TMS-EEG; user would need to negotiate per-PI.
